## beamsim v0.3.1

Patch release: a single physics fix to the knife-edge-diffraction (KED) blockage attenuation, plus a follow-up to the [MATLAB-parity audit](../../docs/MATLAB_PARITY.md) that resolves the `W_HIGH` and MCMD slot-7 questions.

### Fixed: KED attenuation now uses the physical Fresnel-zone scale

The previous KED attenuation formula in `channel._ked_attenuation_db` hardcoded `lambda_eff = 0.4` — a magic constant with no physical motivation — and used the wrong algebraic form `(π/λ_eff − 1)/cos`. TR 38.901 §7.6.4.1 and Eq 3.17 of the predecessor MSc report specify `π·r/λ · (1/cos − 1)` inside the square root, where `r` is the blocker depth and `λ` is the carrier wavelength.

The corrected formula:

- Threads the carrier frequency through `BlockageState.wavelength_m` (computed as `c / fc_hz` from `ChannelParams.fc_hz`).
- Adds `BlockageState.blocker_radius_m`, defaulting to 10 m to match the predecessor's `blockage.m`.
- Computes `scale = π · r / λ` (≈ 2940 at 28 GHz with a 10 m blocker, three orders of magnitude larger than the previous 6.85), so KED attenuation depth at oblique incidence is now physically meaningful and scales correctly with both wavelength and blocker depth.

### Audit follow-up: W_HIGH and MCMD slot-7 resolved as already-thesis-faithful

The [MATLAB-parity audit](../../docs/MATLAB_PARITY.md) had flagged Python's `W_HIGH = (0.16, 0.36, 0.49)` and Python's three-criterion MCMD as divergences from the MATLAB code. After reading the predecessor MSc thesis (Fig 5.26 pie charts and the surrounding text), both turn out to be cases where **Python is faithful to the published thesis** and the MATLAB code is from a later un-published re-fit — the evolutionary search (Algorithm 7) uses random initialisation, so re-runs produce different terminal weights. No code change to MCMD was needed; the audit's `Bugs in Python (MATLAB is the correct reference)` category is now empty.

### Test count

248 → 250 passing.
